text:
POST 6  Motive check: Did he gain anything?

Power, wealth, status?
False prophets seek:
  Luxury
  Control
  Dynasties

Muhammad :
  Lived in a mud-brick room
  Often slept on a straw mat
  Died with his armour pawned for food
  Left no inheritance

When offered kingship and wealth, he refused.
Even after ruling Arabia, his lifestyle did not change.
Reminder:
People fake religion to gain the world  Muhammad  abandoned the world when it was offered to him.

The claim that Muhammad  sought worldly gainwealth, power, or prestigeis contradicted by well-attested historical and material evidence from his life.

Despite ruling Madinah and having access to resources unavailable to him earlier in Makkah, the Prophet  continued to live in extreme material simplicity. His dwelling beside the Prophets Mosque was austere, especially when contrasted with the luxury of contemporary Byzantine and Sasanian rulers. This physical reality remains a tangible historical testimony.

Multiple authentic reports establish that his lifestyle was one of consistent moderation and self-denial:

  Chronic material scarcity: ishah reported that the Prophets household in Madinah did not eat their fill for three consecutive nights until his death (Bukhr 6089; Muslim 2970). Even at the time of his passing, there was nothing edible in his home except a small amount of barley (Bukhari 6086, Muslim 5281).

  Voluntary asceticism: This deprivation was not forced by weakness or persecution; it continued unchanged even when wealth and authority were available to him.

  Aversion to wealth at death: During his final illness, the Prophet  insisted that a small amount of gold in his possession be given away in charity immediately, expressing fear of meeting God with any worldly attachment (reported by Amad; authenticated by al-Haytham).

  Eyewitness testimony of companions: Umar ibn al-Khab described finding the Prophet  sleeping on a rough mat that left marks on his body, with only a small measure of barley and a few leaves as provisions. When Umar wept at this contrast with imperial luxury, the Prophet replied: Are you not pleased that they have the world, and we have the Hereafter? (Ibn Mjah; authenticated by al-kim).

  No accumulation or inheritance: He died with his armour held as collateral by a Jewish resident of Madinah for barley (Bukhr 2759). He explicitly declared that prophets do not leave inheritance; whatever they leave is charity (Bukhr 6346; Muslim 1758). Consequently, neither his wives nor his daughter inherited wealth from him.

This patternliving with the bare minimum, dying with the bare minimum, and leaving ones family with the bare minimumis fundamentally inconsistent with the behaviour of a charlatan motivated by self-interest, prestige, or material gain.

This conclusion is not confined to Muslim scholarship. The New Catholic Encyclopedia states:
A case has been made that mere economic gain was the inspiration for Mohammeds religious revolution. That case does not suit the facts as they are known. (The New Catholic Encyclopedia 2003, second edition, vol 10, p52. art. Muhammad.)

Taken collectively, the evidence strongly indicates that Muhammad  did not possess, nor act upon, any worldly interest in his claim to Prophethood.

text:
POST 5:

Someone assessing Muhammads   claim to prophethood faces a stark set of possibilities: either accept that he was truthful and fully trustworthy in conveying what he believed was revealed to him; and that his role as a messenger between humanity and its Creator was genuine; or conclude that he knowingly fabricated his message and orchestrated a sustained deception against those who followed him. In this framing, a claimant to prophethood would be either exceptionally truthful, deliberately fraudulent, or sincerely mistaken.

If Muhammads  honesty is rejected, the alternative is to portray him as an unparalleled impostor: inventing portions of the Quran in response to unfolding events, staging words and actions to mimic revelation, and maintaining an elaborate performance of devotion and sincerity. This would also imply consistent deception in private and public life, such as toward his closest Companions, in everyday dealings, and even within his household, by presenting an appearance of integrity while concealing calculated dishonesty.

Even those who denied his message struggled, deep down, to regard him as a liar; their refusal was rooted instead in resistance to his call to monotheism and to leaving the religion of their ancestors, not in doubts about his personal integrity. The Quran reflects this distinction: We certainly know that what they say grieves you It is not you they accuse of lying, but it is Allahs signs that the wrongdoers deny (Quran 6:33). Accordingly, their stance was less an accusation of falsehood than a rejection of his prophethood driven by obstinacy.

               

Paul Casanova, a French Orientalist states: 
'Before entering into the heart of the question, I wish to state at once that I reject a priori any theory tending to suspect Mohammeds sincerity. No doubt one may be led to believe that, in his later years, the necessities of politics led him to present his personal ideas as divine revelations, so as to secure better obedience. But that must not cast doubt on his sincerity; a modern psychologist may see in it auto-suggestion, and it is contrary to every scientific mind to assert, without proofs, that there was imposture and calculation. The whole history of the Arab Prophet proves that his character was positive, serious, and loyal. Even when his absolute power was acknowledged, he knew how to listen to advice, admit his faults, and correct them'
(Casanova, P. (1911). Mohammed et la fin du monde: tude critique sur lIslam primitif. Paris: Geuthner. P5)

When Muhammad  was instructed to summon his close kin to Islam, he chose a public, familiar setting and addressed the Quraysh clans from Mount af, calling them by name. He then tested the credibility they already attributed to him: If I told you that horsemen were in the valley preparing to attack you, would you believe me? They replied that they would, because they had only ever known him to be truthful. (a al-Bukhr, hadith 4523).

He met lifes hardships with a calm spirit and turned away from temptation with unwavering resolve. He deliberately chose a demanding road marked by isolation and painful trials, driven by the task of conveying what he believed to be the truth: calling people away from inherited idolatry and moral corruption, and gathering them around virtue and piety.
How can that portrait be made consistent with the opposing claim sometimes advanced by criticsthat he was a liar, animated by deception and emptied of genuine goodness? The two pictures point in opposite directions; holding them together as equally true creates a contradiction that needs explaining rather than assertion.

It is strongly corroborative of Mahomets sincerity that the earliest converts to Islam were not only of upright character, but his own bosom friends and people of
his household; who, intimately acquainted with his private life, could not fail otherwise to have detected those discrepancies which ever more or less exist between the professions of the hypocritical deceiver abroad and his actions at home. (Muir, W. (1877). Life of Mahomet: From Original Sources. London:Smith, Elder, and Co. p60)

Heracliusdescribed as perceptive and shrewdquestioned Ab Sufyn while Ab Sufyn was still a non-believer and openly hostile to Muhammad , and asked whether Muhammad  had ever been accused of lying. Ab Sufyn replied that he had not, so Heraclius reasoned that someone who would not lie to people would be unlikely to fabricate a lie about God. (Reported in a al-Bukhr, hadith 4278, and a Muslim, hadith 1773.)

To maintain that Muhammad  was lying, one must believe:
-He fabricated the Quran while maintaining internal consistency for 23 years
-He knowingly condemned lying while being historys greatest liar
-He endured persecution, exile, hunger, and war for a deception
-He lived modestly despite absolute authority
-He died without wealth, dynasty, or inheritance

Critical Question:
What liar chooses suffering when power, wealth, and compromise were repeatedly offered?

text:
POST 4  Moral character

Moral uprightness as evidence

Before prophethood, Muhammad  was universally known as:
al-diq al-Amn  The Truthful, The Trustworthy

This reputation was not given by followers 
It was acknowledged by his enemies.

Key question:
Would divine guidance be entrusted to someone morally corrupt?
Across every dimension:
Honesty
Mercy
Patience
Humility
Self-restraint

Muhammad  exhibited consistent moral excellence, privately and publicly.

Even his closest attendant (Anas ibn Mlik) reported:
By Allah, I served him for seven or nine yearsI do not know that he said for something I did not do: Why did you not do such and such? (Muslim 2310)

When the feet of the Prophet were swollen from
standing in worship, it was said to him, O Messenger of Allah, Allah has forgiven you all your past sins. He replied, So should I not be a thankful servant? (Bukhari 4557, Muslim 2819,  wording from Ahmad 18114)

His companion Anas reports: ''There was no person more beloved to them than the Messenger of Allah. When they saw him, they would not stand, as they knew he disliked it (Tirmidh i 335)

When Ab Hurayrah requested that he place a sheet
over the mat that left a mark on the Prophets back, he replied, And what connection do I have with the world? In relation to this world, I am just like a traveller who took shade under a tree, and then he got up and left.'  (Tirmidh 2377)

When Makkah was conquered, Ab Bakr carried
his own father so that he would give his pledge to the Messenger of God. He said, Why could you not leave the old man at his house, so that we would be the ones to visit him? (Amad 27001, Ibn ibbn 7208, and kim 4363)

The Prophet () said, "When I start the prayer I intend to prolong it, but on hearing the cries of a child, I cut short the prayer because I know that the cries of the child will incite its mother's passions." (Bukhr 709); and Muslim 192).

Abdullh ibn Masd said, We were with the Messenger of Allah on a journey. We saw a desert lark with two chicks. We took the chicks. The lark came and apped her wings furiously. When the Messenger came, he said, Who has grieved this bird over its chicks? Return the chicks to her.  (Ab Dwd, 5268)

Frauds do not maintain moral consistency across decades, hardship, power, and intimacy.

POST 3  Preservation: Can we even know who Muhammad  was?

Is his life historically accessible?

A common objection is:
Islamic history was fabricated later.
But this claim collapses under scrutiny.

Muhammad  is the most extensively documented religious figure of antiquity.

Why?
-The Quran preserves real-time responses to events
-His life (srah) is preserved through multiple independent chains
-Muslim scholars developed an unparalleled system of narrator and text criticism (hadith)

Islamic historiography surpassed other approaches in rigor.

Conclusion:If Muhammad  cannot be known historically, no ancient figure can.

text:
POST 2  How prophethood is tested

Extraordinary claims require transparent evaluation.

Prophethood is not about charisma or popularity. It is about truthful transmission of revelation.

Any claim to prophethood must satisfy at least these two conditions:
1.  Truthfulness of the claimant
2.  Irrefutable evidence for the claim

We therefore ask:
  Was Muhammad  known to lie?
  Did he provide demonstrable evidence for his claims?
  Did his life align with what he preached?

Crucially, neutral history leaves us only three options:
  Muhammad  was profoundly honest (i.e. a genuine prophet of God)
  Or he was sincerely deluded
  Or he was the greatest deceiver in human history

There is no other reasonable position. The idea that he is a legendary figure shaped by later followers, is not worth entertaining.

text:
How do we critically test a claim to Prophethood?

Series Introduction
Many people ask:Why should anyone believe Muhammad  was a true prophet of God?

This series does not begin with blind faith. It begins withcritical evaluation.

Any serious assessment of prophethood must answer three preliminary questions:

Does God exist?
Does God communicate with humanity?
Are miracles and revelation logically possible?

If the answer to these is yes, then the question becomes empirical and historical:

Did Muhammad  truthfully convey a message from God, or did he fabricate it?

In this series, we will examine his claim usingpublic evidence, not theological presuppositions.

What this series will cover:
Each post will address one critical criterion used by historians and philosophers when evaluating prophetic claims:
-Preservation of Muhammads  life and message
-His moral character before and after prophethood
-His honesty and reputation among allies and enemies
-Psychological sincerity vs deception or delusion
-Worldly motives  power, wealth, status?
-The Quran as a public, testable miracle
-Historical miracles and mass testimony
-Addressing common objections

Outcome:
Either Muhammad  was:

-A deliberate fraud
-A sincerely deluded man
-Or a genuine prophet of God

Onlyoneof these explanations survives critical scrutiny.

text:
"Follow the Dall, Not the Muft - 'Allmah Muqbil bin Hdi, Allah have mercy on him

al-Allmah Muqbil bin Had al-Wdi, Allah have mercy on him, was asked regarding the befitting stance of a person on a fatwa from a single scholar; specifically in matters of disagreement. Should it suffice him or should he ask as many scholars as possible, and then follow a fatwa from their fatwa? Is this acceptable and is it negligent to ask only a single scholar? 

His answer:

What I advise the brother with, if he is a student of knowledge, is that he should research in the books, and should diligently attempt to find the truth himself from the books of the people of knowledge, and the affair is easy, Allah willing. And if this is not easy, then what matters is the ease of the heart. So if the heart is assured by asking the first scholar [whose answers come] with proof [then this is acceptable]. 

You must ask for proof, because Allah says, Follow what has been revealed to you from your Lord and do not follow other than Him any awliy. Little do you remember.  [Surat al-Arf: 3]. If he brings proof from the book of Allah and from the sunnah of the Messenger of Allah, sallallhu alaihi wa sallam, and you have understood the proof, then suffice yourself with this, or then you can ask another. And be sure that the proof is not abrogated, or that it is not a proof for what it is being used for, as it may be that the muft takes to a madh-hab, and is giving a fatwa which agrees with his madh-hab.

Ijbatus-Sil, p.638
Translation: Abu Hafsah Kashiff Khan"

text:
" A night porter encouraged a 12-year old-girl to use a sex toy on herself and even asked her to come and stay in a hotel room with him while on holiday in Cornwall so he could rape her

Jonathan Pound started engaging in communication with a child when he joined the Kik messaging app which is known to be targeted at and used by children and teenagers.

He contacted a girl aged 12 and soon the conversation took a sexualised turn in nature.

A sentencing hearing at Truro Crown Court yesterday (Monday, April 27) heard how the conversation - even when Pound knew perfectly well that the girl was 12 years old - went from flirtatious to disgusting.

In 15 exchanges over five weeks, Pound, 44, from Station Road in Grampound Road, between Truro and St Austell, asked the girl if she was into older men, if she had a nice body and even asked her to send him pictures of her breasts and genitals.

The court heard that on one occasion he asked her to go in the house, locate a sex toy and insert it into her vagina and then he sent her a picture of his penis.

It was when he tried to encourage the girl to meet up at a hotel room he would book and asked her to come alone so staff would not bat an eyelid but would think she was his own daughter, that Devon and Cornwall Police, who had run the fake-girl account as a paedo-hunting decoy, pounced.

Pound was arrested and charged with one count of inciting a girl under 13 to engage in sexual activity and another of engaging in sexual communication with a child in connection with the incident in February/March 2024.

The 44-year-old dad of two young children later pleaded guilty to the charges.

His barrister said the incident happened two years ago and had been a one-off and Pound had not offended either before or since.

She said that Pound denied having any sexual interest in children but admitted that what he did was wrong, adding that he had been lonely at the time with no-one to speak to about his mental health issues.

His Honour Judge Simon Carr said Pound had intended to use Kik because it is targeted at and used by children and teens. 

He said Pound's conversation with the fake child were entirely sexualised and abhorrent in nature.

Judge Carr said Pound's offences were so serious an immediate custodial sentence was unavoidable.

He sentenced Pound to three years in prison. He will serve half before he is eligible to be released on licence. 

He will also be on the sex offenders' register for life and a sexual harm prevention order restricting his access to the internet was imposed for 10 years. 

Source UK Database."

 Aid / Crossings

 Imad Miqdad

 Occupied Palestine / East Jerusalem

 Dr Hussam Abu Safiya

 Knesset Death Penalty Bill

 Core Closing Line

" A primary school teaching assistant groomed a 10-year-old child into sending sexual videos on Snapchat. 

Charlie Territt, who worked at Streatham Wells Primary School, was sentenced to seven years in prison on Thursday (April 23) by a judge who described him as having a perverted fixation with the victim. 

Prosecutor Bhavin Patel said that throughout 2024, Territt engaged in late-night conversations with a vulnerable child on Snapchat. 

The 32-year-old sent sexual videos of himself and incited the child to send similar videos in return. 

Noticing a change in behaviour, the victim's elder sibling inspected the Snapchat account and discovered Territts vile messages. 

The abuse was reported to the police and on January 3 last year, police attended Territts home in South Bermondsey where they seized several phones and sex toys. 

Mr Patel said: "Whilst police were inside his home, a car left the property. The driver and occupant were the defendants brother and cousin. The car was stopped and two more mobile phones were seized. 

Territt denied the abuse in interview and continued to do so after being charged, until an analysis of his phone revealed a sexual video of the victim as well as indecent images of three other children. 

Facing incontrovertible evidence, according to Judge Silas Reid, Territt then pleaded guilty to:

- Causing or inciting a child to engage in sexual activity,

However, the defence said his client denied being a paedophile and that it was an attraction to this child as opposed to a wider interest. 

When Judge Reid questioned why, if he is not a paedophile, Territt had sexual images of other children too, Mr Waller said there were not as many images as typically seen in paedophile cases.

The damage you wreaked by your behaviour will no doubt last many years if not forever. 

Judge Reid sentenced Territt to seven years imprisonment.

text:
" Sorry folks, another grim one.

 A diaper fetishist" with a grotesque fixation on rooting through bins for used nappies has been jailed for a total of 2.5 years for doing the same thing again.

Abbie Taylor had been banned from going near bins but was seen by two different people climbing in and searching through their waste, Newcastle Crown Court heard.

Taylor, who identifies as a woman but previously committed offences under the name Martin Tarling and was being held on remand at a male prison, was given a suspended sentence in 2025 for targeting nurseries in South Tyneside.

The 47-year-old from South Shields, whose criminal record had 99 offences on it, has admitted breaching a criminal behaviour order and was jailed for two and a half years.

A three-year order was made in 2023 prohibiting Taylor from attending any nurseries, removing items from bins or wearing incontinence aids over clothing in public, the prosecutor said.

Taylor was originally given a suspended two years sentence in August 2025 for breaching the 2023 order by committing offences at several nurseries which included repeatedly dumping soiled nappies near nurseries and raking through the bins semi-naked.

In December 2025, a woman who lived with her husband, who needed to use adult nappies, found several of his soiled garments scattered around their garden, the court heard.

She believed they might have been blown out by the wind, but later that day heard someone moving her bins and looked out to see Taylor rummaging, the court heard.

The woman banged on the window and shouted, causing Taylor to flee semi-dressed, the court heard.

The following day, the woman saw Taylor going through her bin again, with the defendant leaving with a bag of rubbish, the court heard.

Shortly afterward in December another woman saw Taylor standing in her bin and searching, the prosecutor said.

A previous court hearing was told Taylor was a "diaper fetishist" whose Facebook profile described the defendant as an "adult baby diaper lover".

But in mitigation, a psychiatrist had concluded Taylor 's actions were not sexually motivated, which was accepted by the judge.

The judge said Taylor had a "grotesque obsession" with "rooting around in bins near care homes or nurseries to retrieve soiled nappies or incontinence wear".

"Your conduct is highly anti-social and extremely distressing to people who are affected by it," the judge told Taylor.

Taylor was jailed for 16 months for breaching the 2023 court order and 14 months consecutive for breaching the suspended sentence imposed in August 2025.

Source True Crime Online."

"Mass Kidnappings Across Chechnya! 

Russian occupational forces and FSB-directed units have launched what appears to be a coordinated new wave of mass abductions across occupied Chechnya. Over 100 people are reportedly kidnapped in just the past few days alone, with operations spanning multiple villages and districts.

The Kadyrov-Russian regime continues its hunt for young men. Some of those kidnapped are sent to military enlistment offices and forced to sign contracts with the Ministry of Defence. Families of the kidnapped are being extorted for hundreds of thousands of rubles.

According to NIYSO, one of the abducted residents of the village of Prigorodnoe has died whilst in  custody.  The deceased has been identified as Mimbulatov  a father of two brothers, who was abducted alongside them. Whether he was subjected to torture remains unclear, as given his advanced age (60+), even the stress of the situation alone could have led to death.

Today, a large number of people who knew the Mimbulatov family came to the funeral in Prigorodnoe. No investigation into the cause of death or criminal proceedings against the officers involved will, of course, take place. The Kadyrov puppet administration continues to terrorise and kill Chechens,"
